summary: PR #2499 refines lbarq decompilation by replacing opaque lbArq global padding with a typed 10-node pool, introducing lbArqState enum names for list/state slots, updating state uses in lbarq.c, keeping lbArq_80014ABC out-of-line with a narrow dont_inline pragma, and adding PAD_STACK in lbArq_80014BD0. The PR body claims 100% matches for lbArq_80014ABC and lbArq_80014D2C and partial improvements for lbArq_80014AC4 and lbArq_80014BD0; the bot report independently records lbArq_80014BD0 improving from 93.97% to 96.55%. The PR was closed with no merged_at in the supplied metadata.

key_files.role: Implements lbarq matching/type cleanup: enum state uses replace raw list indices and numeric states, lbArq_80014ABC return type becomes lbArqState with a dont_inline pragma block, lbArq_80014BD0 adds PAD_STACK(16), and initialization uses global->nodes instead of casting the global object.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbarq.c hunks changing state constants, global->nodes, PAD_STACK(16), and #pragma dont_inline around lbArq_80014ABC

key_files.role: Defines lbArqState enum, changes lbArqNode.state from u32 to lbArqState, replaces lbArqGlobal padding with lbArqNode nodes[10], and updates lbArq_80014ABC prototype.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbarq.h enum lbArqState and lbArqGlobal node pool changes
human_pr_text: 'add concrete lbArq state/list types and replace the opaque global padding with the 10-node pool'

what_changed.text: Added lbArqState enum values LB_ARQ_STATE_FREE, LB_ARQ_STATE_PENDING, and LB_ARQ_STATE_DONE and used them for list indexing and node state assignments instead of raw 0/1/2 constants.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbarq.c replacements of list[0], list[1], list[2] and state 0/1/2 with enum constants

what_changed.text: Changed lbArqNode.state and lbArq_80014ABC return type from u32 to lbArqState, aligning the accessor and field type with the recovered state names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbarq.h state field and lbArq_80014ABC prototype

what_changed.text: Replaced lbArqGlobal's opaque u8 pad[0x1E0] with lbArqNode nodes[10], and changed initialization from casting the global object to using global->nodes.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Summary states replacement of opaque global padding with 10-node pool

what_changed.text: Kept lbArq_80014ABC out-of-line using a scoped #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop block.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbarq.c pragma block around lbArq_80014ABC
human_pr_text: Summary says keep lbArq_80014ABC out-of-line

what_changed.text: Added PAD_STACK(16) in lbArq_80014BD0 and retained PAD_STACK(8) in lbArq_80014D2C.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbarq.c PAD_STACK(16) added in lbArq_80014BD0
raw_diff: lbarq.c PAD_STACK(8) present in lbArq_80014D2C
human_pr_text: Summary mentions improving lbArq_80014BD0 stack/codegen

what_changed.text: PR body reports checkdiff runs for lbArq_80014ABC, lbArq_80014AC4, lbArq_80014BD0, and lbArq_80014D2C, plus configure/ninja; the loaded slice does not include command output.
Bot report records one unmatched-item improvement for main/melee/lb/lbarq lbArq_80014BD0, from 93.97% to 96.55%, +10 bytes.

smart_moves.text: Typed the recovered global layout directly as nodes[10] rather than continuing to access the pool through padding and casts.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbArqGlobal u8 pad[0x1E0] -> lbArqNode nodes[10]

smart_moves.text: Converted magic state numbers into named enum constants while preserving their numeric values and list-index role.
Scoped the dont_inline pragma narrowly around lbArq_80014ABC instead of applying a broad file-wide pragma.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: #pragma push, #pragma dont_inline on, lbArq_80014ABC, #pragma pop

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a global's opaque padding is repeatedly treated as a homogeneous object pool, replacing the padding with a typed array can improve source clarity and matching without relying on casts.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: 'replace the opaque global padding with the 10-node pool'
raw_diff: lbArqGlobal nodes[10] and global->nodes use

decomp_lessons.lesson: For state/list-index values that are verified by local code behavior, an enum can make list transitions readable while preserving the exact numeric indices needed for matching.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: 'name the verified ARQ node states used by lbarq'
raw_diff: LB_ARQ_STATE_FREE/PENDING/DONE used as list indexes and state values

decomp_lessons.lesson: Narrow stack/codegen tactics such as PAD_STACK and dont_inline should be tied to explicit matching claims and kept tightly scoped.

naming_conventions.name: lbArqState
naming_conventions.convention: Subsystem-prefixed enum type naming follows lbArq* naming already used by lbArqNode, lbArqGlobal, and lbArqCallback.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: surrounding typedefs lbArqCallback, lbArqNode, lbArqGlobal

naming_conventions.name: LB_ARQ_STATE_FREE, LB_ARQ_STATE_PENDING, LB_ARQ_STATE_DONE
naming_conventions.convention: Uppercase subsystem-prefixed state constants encode the ARQ node list/state roles and preserve values 0, 1, and 2.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: replacements in lbarq.c list and state assignments

naming_conventions.name: nodes
naming_conventions.convention: A recovered object-pool field is named by its element role rather than left as pad storage.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Scoped #pragma dont_inline around lbArq_80014ABC to keep a small state accessor out-of-line.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Loaded evidence includes the author's claimed checkdiff command but not checkdiff output.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK(16) in lbArq_80014BD0 for stack/codegen improvement.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The PR body states remaining drift in saved-register allocation and one extra move around the free-list head; the function is not claimed as fully matched.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use typed enum constants as array indices to preserve list-address behavior while improving readability.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: lbArq_80014AC4 remaining drift described as one first-list address calculation order
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: lbArq_80014AC4 remains a partial match per the PR body.

follow_up_queries.purpose: Assess whether a source update is warranted for narrowly scoped dont_inline exceptions.

follow_up_queries.query: Search for PAD_STACK uses in src/melee/lb and PR bodies that cite stack/codegen improvements.
follow_up_queries.purpose: Collect evidence for how PAD_STACK is justified and documented in reviewable source.

follow_up_queries.query: Search lbarq follow-up PRs for lbArq_80014AC4 or lbArq_80014BD0 reaching 100%.
follow_up_queries.purpose: Track whether the remaining drift described in this PR was later resolved.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover typed object pools from opaque global padding when code consistently treats the storage as nodes.
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2499 changed lbArqGlobal from u8 pad[0x1E0] to lbArqNode nodes[10] and updated initialization to use global->nodes, matching the PR summary's claim that the opaque global padding represented a 10-node pool.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Name verified state/list-index values with subsystem-prefixed enum constants.
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2499 introduced lbArqState with LB_ARQ_STATE_FREE/PENDING/DONE and replaced raw 0/1/2 state and list-index uses in lbarq.c.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: state and list index replacements
human_pr_text: 'name the verified ARQ node states used by lbarq'

source_update_candidates.title: Consider explicit guidance for typed object-pool recovery from padding
source_update_candidates.text: This PR is an example of replacing opaque global padding with a typed fixed-size pool when local code and initialization identify the element type and count. Curator may consider whether typed-fields-over-pointer-math or data-sections-and-tu-splits should mention typed object-pool recovery as a reviewable cleanup pattern when backed by local evidence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbArqGlobal u8 pad[0x1E0] -> lbArqNode nodes[10]
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded standards already prefer typed fields over raw storage; this PR may provide a concrete source-owned example but should be curated rather than automatically promoted.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider documenting narrow dont_inline pragma exceptions for accessor matching
source_update_candidates.text: This PR used #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around a small state accessor, with the PR body claiming lbArq_80014ABC reached 100%. Curator may decide whether avoid-pragmas-register-asm should include this as an example of a tightly scoped exception that still needs verifier evidence.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: lbArq_80014ABC 100% and verification command list
source_update_candidates.reason: The existing standards caution against pragmas; this slice supports only a candidate example and lacks raw checkdiff output.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR's claimed 100% matches as independently verified results beyond author-stated PR-body evidence; loaded raw checkdiff outputs are absent.
